definition: The second-truth expression naming the origin of dissatisfaction.
notes: Use this record when the compact compound dukkha-samudaya is named directly rather than unfolded as the full noble-truth formula. Keep the compound aligned with the headword defaults for dukkha and samudaya, and do not let the term drift back into suffering-language by habit.

example_phrases.translation: this is the noble truth of the origin of dissatisfaction
example_phrases.notes: Compact truth-label use for the second truth.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'origin of dissatisfaction' when the compact second-truth compound itself is named; prefer the full noble-truth formula when the sentence explicitly uses the longer construction.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not let the compound drift into suffering-language or replace it with a looser paraphrase when the truth-label itself is under discussion.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the second-truth compound from drifting away from the repository defaults for dukkha and samudaya.
